$comment: CodeAnt AI review instructions for Nexus-HEMS-Dash. Docs: https://docs.codeant.ai/pull_request/customize/global_repo_config . These shape review comments; they do NOT auto-apply changes. Advisory posture — see docs/runbooks/codeant-ai-integration.md.

instructions.description: React 19 with the React Compiler is enabled. Do NOT recommend adding manual useMemo/useCallback/memo unless the compiler provably cannot handle the case. Never propose Redux, MobX, or other state libraries; the app uses Zustand. Never propose Tailwind v3 syntax (use Tailwind v4 @theme / @import 'tailwindcss').
Every user-visible string must use t(). Any new key must be added to BOTH apps/web/src/locales/en.ts and apps/web/src/locales/de.ts (German is the fallback). Flag missing translations.

WCAG 2.2 AA is mandatory. Require type="button" on all non-submit buttons and aria-hidden on decorative icons. Prefer the branded UI primitives (ChoiceCardGroup, SelectField, Disclosure, SgReadyModeSelector) over raw native controls.

SAFETY-CRITICAL: adapters, command-safety, circuit-breaker, and energy controllers control real electrical hardware. Surface risks for human review; NEVER suggest silent edits to control logic, hardware commands, rate limiting, or safety guardrails. Validate hardware commands via Zod schema + rate limits + audit trail.

Express 5 backend. Validate every incoming datapoint with Zod safeParse before use and route failures to the Dead-Letter Queue. Auth (requireJWT), rate limits, and the OCPP mTLS proxy are security-sensitive — request explicit human review rather than proposing auto-fixes.
packages/shared-types holds Zod schemas + inferred types consumed by BOTH api and web. Any change is potentially breaking across packages — always call out cross-package compatibility.
